Module com.trifasico.x50zo
Package com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players

Class MachineStrategy

java.lang.Object
com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players.MachineStrategy
public class MachineStrategy extends Object
A scoring-based implementation that evaluates each card in the machine's hand and picks the one with the highest score.

Scoring rules (applied in order, scores are additive)

  1. Unplayable card → score set to SCORE_UNPLAYABLE (−1000). Immediately skipped; no other rules apply.
  2. Aggressive play: the card's resolved value is the highest possible among all playable cards in hand → SCORE_AGGRESSIVE (+30). Aims to push the sum as high as possible, cornering the next player.
  3. Neutral nine: rank is Rank.NINE (value 0) and no additive card exists → SCORE_NINE_NEUTRAL (+20). Preserves the sum when no safe positive card is available.
  4. Subtractive play: rank is J/Q/K (value −10) and no additive or neutral option is playable → SCORE_SUBTRACT (+10). Last resort; lowers the sum but also gives opponents more room.

Ties are broken by the order in which cards appear in the hand (first highest-scoring card wins). The algorithm is O(n²) in hand size, which is negligible given the maximum hand size of 4.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Yostin Ramirez, Lesly Zapata, Joseph Terreros

  • Method Details

    • chooseCardIndex

      public int chooseCardIndex(List<Card> hand, GameState gameState)

      Scores every card in hand according to the class-level scoring rules and returns the index of the card with the highest score.
